paper.title: A Game of Thrones — Prologue
paper.author: George R. R. Martin
paper.reader_mode: spoiler-free close reading; no full Chinese translation

blocks.original_text: We should start back,” Gared urged as the woods began to grow dark around them. “The wildlings are dead.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Gared催促队伍返回，并断言他们追踪的野人已经死亡。夜色与“死人”从第一句就建立危险气氛。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 开篇没有解释世界观，而让“死人”在普通争执中出现；读者先感到不安，再逐步理解危险。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** 三人正在长城以北追踪野人。`wildlings` 是长城外人群的称呼，并不等于超自然生物。

blocks.original_text: “Do the dead frighten you?” Ser Waymar Royce asked with just the hint of a smile.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar以带笑的反问试探Gared是否害怕死人。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段继续通过军衔、经验和恐惧之间的冲突塑造三人关系，并延迟揭示威胁。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Gared did not rise to the bait. He was an old man, past fifty, and he had seen the lordlings come and go. “Dead is dead,” he said. “We have no business with the dead.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Gared拒绝被激怒，凭经验坚持死者不值得继续调查。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `Dead is dead` 的重复看似坚定，也暴露Gared正在用常识压住无法言说的恐惧。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Are they dead?” Royce asked softly. “What proof have we?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar追问死亡是否得到证实，表现出他对证据的执着。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段继续通过军衔、经验和恐惧之间的冲突塑造三人关系，并延迟揭示威胁。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Will saw them,” Gared said. “If he says they are dead, that’s proof enough for me.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Gared相信Will的侦察判断，说明Will在追踪方面很可靠。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段继续通过军衔、经验和恐惧之间的冲突塑造三人关系，并延迟揭示威胁。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Will had known they would drag him into the quarrel sooner or later. He wished it had been later rather than sooner. “My mother told me that dead men sing no songs,” he put in.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will被迫加入争论，只能引用一句民间俗语缓和局面。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段继续通过军衔、经验和恐惧之间的冲突塑造三人关系，并延迟揭示威胁。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Will处在两名地位更高者的争论之间；俗语来自母亲，反映普通人的生活智慧。

blocks.original_text: “My wet nurse said the same thing, Will,” Royce replied. “Never believe anything you hear at a woman’s tit. There are things to be learned even from the dead.” His voice echoed, too loud in the twilit forest.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar轻蔑地否定乳母的俗话，认为死人同样能提供线索。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Waymar的粗俗与轻蔑建立了阶级冲突：他掌握军衔，却尚未取得老兵的尊敬。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “We have a long ride before us,” Gared pointed out. “Eight days, maybe nine. And night is falling.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Gared从返程距离和天色出发，再次提出撤退的现实理由。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段继续通过军衔、经验和恐惧之间的冲突塑造三人关系，并延迟揭示威胁。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Ser Waymar Royce glanced at the sky with disinterest. “It does that every day about this time. Are you unmanned by the dark, Gared?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar嘲笑他怕黑，把审慎解释成怯懦。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段继续通过军衔、经验和恐惧之间的冲突塑造三人关系，并延迟揭示威胁。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Will could see the tightness around Gared’s mouth, the barely suppressed anger in his eyes under the thick black hood of his cloak. Gared had spent forty years in the Night’s Watch, man and boy, and he was not accustomed to being made light of. Yet it was more than that. Under the wounded pride, Will could sense something else in the older man. You could taste it; a nervous tension that came perilous close to fear.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will看出Gared既愤怒又恐惧；后者的资历让这种恐惧格外可信。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 叙事严格贴近Will的观察；我们不是直接知道Gared害怕，而是从表情与身体反应推断。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Gared在守夜人服役四十年。老兵异常恐惧本身就是重要警报。

blocks.original_text: Will shared his unease. He had been four years on the Wall. The first time he had been sent beyond, all the old stories had come rushing back, and his bowels had turned to water. He had laughed about it afterward. He was a veteran of a hundred rangings by now, and the endless dark wilderness that the southron called the haunted forest had no more terrors for him.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will回忆自己已习惯长城外的巡逻，本以为那些旧故事不再能吓到他。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 先说旧故事已失去力量，再用 `Until tonight` 推翻它，是典型的恐怖叙事转折。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** `the Wall` 是守夜人的边境据点核心；`rangings` 指越过长城执行的巡逻侦察。

blocks.original_text: Until tonight. Something was different tonight. There was an edge to this darkness that made his hackles rise. Nine days they had been riding, north and northwest and then north again, farther and farther from the Wall, hard on the track of a band of wildling raiders. Each day had been worse than the day that had come before it. Today was the worst of all. A cold wind was blowing out of the north, and it made the trees rustle like living things. All day, Will had felt as though something were watching him, something cold and implacable that loved him not. Gared had felt it too. Will wanted nothing so much as to ride hellbent for the safety of the Wall, but that was not a feeling to share with your commander.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 但今晚的黑暗异常压迫；风、树声和被窥视感让两名老手都想逃回长城。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 自然景物被写成有生命、有敌意的存在，恐惧还没有实体，却已经控制感官。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Especially not a commander like this one.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 短句补充：他们尤其不愿向Waymar这样的指挥官承认恐惧。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段继续通过军衔、经验和恐惧之间的冲突塑造三人关系，并延迟揭示威胁。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Ser Waymar Royce was the youngest son of an ancient house with too many heirs. He was a handsome youth of eighteen, grey-eyed and graceful and slender as a knife. Mounted on his huge black destrier, the knight towered above Will and Gared on their smaller garrons. He wore black leather boots, black woolen pants, black moleskin gloves, and a fine supple coat of gleaming black ringmail over layers of black wool and boiled leather. Ser Waymar had been a Sworn Brother of the Night’s Watch for less than half a year, but no one could say he had not prepared for his vocation. At least insofar as his wardrobe was concerned.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 本段介绍Waymar的贵族出身、年龄、坐骑和精良装备，并暗示他经验不足。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 坐骑和服装不是装饰性清单：它们把贵族身份、经济差距和“不适合密林实战”同时写出来。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Waymar来自古老贵族家族，作为幼子缺乏主要继承机会；加入守夜人可能为他提供独立身份。最后一点是基于本段的合理推断。

blocks.original_text: His cloak was his crowning glory; sable, thick and black and soft as sin. “Bet he killed them all himself, he did,” Gared told the barracks over wine, “twisted their little heads off, our mighty warrior.” They had all shared the laugh.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 他的奢华黑斗篷曾被同袍嘲笑，说明他尚未赢得真正的威望。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `soft as sin` 让斗篷兼具诱惑与不祥；后来它也会成为视觉上最醒目的目标。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: It is hard to take orders from a man you laughed at in your cups, Will reflected as he sat shivering atop his garron. Gared must have felt the same.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will意识到，私下嘲笑过Waymar使服从他的命令变得更难。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段继续通过军衔、经验和恐惧之间的冲突塑造三人关系，并延迟揭示威胁。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Mormont said as we should track them, and we did,” Gared said. “They’re dead. They shan’t trouble us no more. There’s hard riding before us. I don’t like this weather. If it snows, we could be a fortnight getting back, and snow’s the best we can hope for. Ever seen an ice storm, my lord?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Gared援引任务已经完成、天气恶化和返程艰难等理由继续劝返。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段继续通过军衔、经验和恐惧之间的冲突塑造三人关系，并延迟揭示威胁。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Mormont 是给他们下达追踪任务的上级；本段不需要知道更多身份细节。

blocks.original_text: The lordling seemed not to hear him. He studied the deepening twilight in that half-bored, half-distracted way he had. Will had ridden with the knight long enough to understand that it was best not to interrupt him when he looked like that. “Tell me again what you saw, Will. All the details. Leave nothing out.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar无视情绪争执，要求Will重新准确描述现场。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Waymar并非完全无能。他要求复述细节，显示出调查意识，只是傲慢妨碍他理解老兵的直觉。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Will had been a hunter before he joined the Night’s Watch. Well, a poacher in truth. Mallister freeriders had caught him red-handed in the Mallisters’ own woods, skinning one of the Mallisters’ own bucks, and it had been a choice of putting on the black or losing a hand. No one could move through the woods as silent as Will, and it had not taken the black brothers long to discover his talent.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will原是偷猎者，被迫在断手与加入守夜人之间选择；他的林地本领因此成为队伍所需的技能。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 守夜人吸纳罪犯，并让其过去的技能服务边境；这是制度功能，也是Will身份不安的来源。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** `take the black` 指加入守夜人。Will因偷猎面临断手刑罚，选择以服役替代。

blocks.original_text: “The camp is two miles farther on, over that ridge, hard beside a stream,” Will said. “I got close as I dared. There’s eight of them, men and women both. No children I could see. They put up a lean-to against the rock. The snow’s pretty well covered it now, but I could still make it out. No fire burning, but the firepit was still plain as day. No one moving. I watched a long time. No living man ever lay so still.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will详述野人营地：八名成年人静止不动、没有火，姿态像死者。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 尸体没有外伤、火已熄灭、姿势异常，这些细节构成一个暂时无法由常识解释的谜。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Did you see any blood?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar首先询问是否有血迹。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Well, no,” Will admitted.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will承认没有看见血。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Did you see any weapons?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar继续询问武器，像是在系统排除遭袭的可能。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Some swords, a few bows. One man had an axe. Heavy-looking, double-bladed, a cruel piece of iron. It was on the ground beside him, right by his hand.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will说明武器仍在死者手边，现场不像发生过搏斗。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 武器仍触手可及，削弱了“遭普通敌人袭击”的解释。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Did you make note of the position of the bodies?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar进一步追问尸体姿势。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Will shrugged. “A couple are sitting up against the rock. Most of them on the ground. Fallen, like.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will描述有人倚石而坐、其余倒地，坚持他们是倒下而非休息。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Or sleeping,” Royce suggested.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar提出另一解释：他们也许只是睡着了。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Fallen,” Will insisted. “There’s one woman up an ironwood, half-hid in the branches. A far-eyes.” He smiled thinly. “I took care she never saw me. When I got closer, I saw that she wasn’t moving neither.” Despite himself, he shivered.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will提到树上的女哨兵也完全不动；他的颤抖表明自己并不相信那只是睡眠。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 树上的哨兵本应最警觉，她的静止让“所有人只是睡着”显得更不可能。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “You have a chill?” Royce asked.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar察觉Will发冷。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Some,” Will muttered. “The wind, m’lord.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will把颤抖归因于风，隐藏真正的恐惧。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: The young knight turned back to his grizzled man-at-arms. Frostfallen leaves whispered past them, and Royce’s destrier moved restlessly. “What do you think might have killed these men, Gared?” Ser Waymar asked casually. He adjusted the drape of his long sable cloak.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar转向经验丰富的Gared，直接问这些人可能如何死亡。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** `man-at-arms` 是受训练、受雇或隶属于贵族武装体系的职业战士，不等同于有爵位的骑士。

blocks.original_text: “It was the cold,” Gared said with iron certainty. “I saw men freeze last winter, and the one before, when I was half a boy. Everyone talks about snows forty foot deep, and how the ice wind comes howling out of the north, but the real enemy is the cold. It steals up on you quieter than Will, and at first you shiver and your teeth chatter and you stamp your feet and dream of mulled wine and nice hot fires. It burns, it does. Nothing burns like the cold. But only for a while. Then it gets inside you and starts to fill you up, and after a while you don’t have the strength to fight it. It’s easier just to sit down or go to sleep. They say you don’t feel any pain toward the end. First you go weak and drowsy, and everything starts to fade, and then it’s like sinking into a sea of warm milk. Peaceful, like.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Gared详细描述冻死的过程：先疼痛，继而麻木、虚弱、嗜睡，最后平静死亡。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 冻死过程被写得逐步而具体，使“寒冷”从天气变成能侵入人体的敌人。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Such eloquence, Gared,” Ser Waymar observed. “I never suspected you had it in you.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar用讽刺口吻称赞Gared的口才。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “I’ve had the cold in me too, lordling.” Gared pulled back his hood, giving Ser Waymar a good long look at the stumps where his ears had been. “Two ears, three toes, and the little finger off my left hand. I got off light. We found my brother frozen at his watch, with a smile on his face.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Gared展示冻伤造成的残缺，并讲到兄弟冻死的经历，证明自己的判断来自亲历。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 伤残是比口头资历更强的证据；Waymar仍不接受，突出知识与权力的错位。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Maester Aemon 曾处理Gared的冻伤；`maester` 可先理解为城堡或组织中的学者、医者与顾问。

blocks.original_text: Ser Waymar shrugged. “You ought dress more warmly, Gared.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar用一句“穿暖些”轻率打发这段惨痛经验。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Gared glared at the lordling, the scars around his ear holes flushed red with anger where Maester Aemon had cut the ears away. “We’ll see how warm you can dress when the winter comes.” He pulled up his hood and hunched over his garron, silent and sullen.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Gared被激怒；Will开始注意到天气与尸体状态之间存在矛盾。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段以侦察细节或推理推进谜团，让常识解释逐渐显得不足。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “If Gared said it was the cold . . . ” Will began.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will试图替Gared辩护，却被Waymar打断。

blocks.original_text: “And how did you find the Wall?”

blocks.original_text: “Weeping,” Will said, frowning. He saw it clear enough, now that the lordling had pointed it out. “They couldn’t have froze. Not if the Wall was weeping. It wasn’t cold enough.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will意识到长城正在融水；若气温足以融冰，就不足以迅速冻死八名有装备的人。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `the Wall was weeping` 是形象化说法：冰墙融水像流泪，同时提供气温证据。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** 长城主体为冰，表面融水能作为温度变化的直观指标。

blocks.original_text: Royce nodded. “Bright lad. We’ve had a few light frosts this past week, and a quick flurry of snow now and then, but surely no cold fierce enough to kill eight grown men. Men clad in fur and leather, let me remind you, with shelter near at hand, and the means of making fire.” The knight’s smile was cocksure. “Will, lead us there. I would see these dead men for myself.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar完成推理，命令Will带路，决意亲眼查看尸体。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 这一推理让Waymar显出聪明和责任感，人物因此不只是单纯的傲慢贵族。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: And then there was nothing to be done for it. The order had been given, and honor bound them to obey.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 命令下达后，荣誉和军纪迫使两名下属服从。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `honor bound them` 表明悲剧并非因为所有人都判断错误，而是正确判断无法战胜等级和职责。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** 守夜人有明确的指挥链与服从义务；个人经验不能自动推翻上级命令。

blocks.original_text: Will went in front, his shaggy little garron picking the way carefully through the undergrowth. A light snow had fallen the night before, and there were stones and roots and hidden sinks lying just under its crust, waiting for the careless and the unwary. Ser Waymar Royce came next, his great black destrier snorting impatiently. The warhorse was the wrong mount for ranging, but try and tell that to the lordling. Gared brought up the rear. The old man-at-arms muttered to himself as he rode.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will领路；积雪掩盖坑洼，而Waymar高大的战马与奢华斗篷都不适合密林。

blocks.original_text: Twilight deepened. The cloudless sky turned a deep purple, the color of an old bruise, then faded to black. The stars began to come out. A half-moon rose. Will was grateful for the light.

blocks.reading_note_zh: 天空像旧伤瘀青，是颜色描写也是暴力预示；黑白意象此后不断加强。

blocks.original_text: Will could feel it. Four years in the Night’s Watch, and he had never been so afraid. What was it?

blocks.original_text: “Wind. Trees rustling. A wolf. Which sound is it that unmans you so, Gared?” When Gared did not answer, Royce slid gracefully from his saddle. He tied the destrier securely to a low-hanging limb, well away from the other horses, and drew his longsword from its sheath. Jewels glittered in its hilt, and the moonlight ran down the shining steel. It was a splendid weapon, castle-forged, and new-made from the look of it. Will doubted it had ever been swung in anger.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar把危险感拆成普通声响并继续嘲笑；随后拔出华丽但未经实战的长剑。

blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** `castle-forged` 强调武器出自条件良好的锻造场所，象征品质与身份，但不保证使用者有经验。

blocks.original_text: Gared’s hood shadowed his face, but Will could see the hard glitter in his eyes as he stared at the knight. For a moment he was afraid the older man would go for his sword. It was a short, ugly thing, its grip discolored by sweat, its edge nicked from hard use, but Will would not have given an iron bob for the lordling’s life if Gared pulled it from its scabbard.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Gared一度像要违抗甚至攻击Waymar，但长久形成的服从最终占了上风。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 这里的对峙把军纪写成一种近乎本能的力量；Gared看见危险，却无法越过身份秩序。

blocks.original_text: Finally Gared looked down. “No fire,” he muttered, low under his breath.

blocks.original_text: Will threaded their way through a thicket, then started up the slope to the low ridge where he had found his vantage point under a sentinel tree. Under the thin crust of snow, the ground was damp and muddy, slick footing, with rocks and hidden roots to trip you up. Will made no sound as he climbed. Behind him, he heard the soft metallic slither of the lordling’s ringmail, the rustle of leaves, and muttered curses as reaching branches grabbed at his longsword and tugged on his splendid sable cloak.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will凭猎人技巧安静攀上山脊，Waymar的盔甲、长剑和斗篷却不断发出声响并被树枝牵扯。

blocks.original_text: The great sentinel was right there at the top of the ridge, where Will had known it would be, its lowest branches a bare foot off the ground. Will slid in underneath, flat on his belly in the snow and the mud, and looked down on the empty clearing below.

blocks.original_text: His heart stopped in his chest. For a moment he dared not breathe. Moonlight shone down on the clearing, the ashes of the firepit, the snow-covered lean-to, the great rock, the little half-frozen stream. Everything was just as it had been a few hours ago.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 营地的火坑、棚屋、岩石与溪流都和几小时前完全一致；这种准确对应让Will确信自己没有找错地方。

blocks.original_text: Ser Waymar looked him over with open disapproval. “I am not going back to Castle Black a failure on my first ranging. We will find these men.” He glanced around. “Up the tree. Be quick about it. Look for a fire.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar担心首次巡逻以失败告终，坚持搜索并命令Will爬树观察火光。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段利用空营地、消失的尸体和人物反应，把预感转化为可观察的异常。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Castle Black 是他们返回后要报告的守夜人据点；Waymar把首次巡逻的成败与个人名誉相连。

blocks.original_text: Will turned away, wordless. There was no use to argue. The wind was moving. It cut right through him. He went to the tree, a vaulting grey-green sentinel, and began to climb. Soon his hands were sticky with sap, and he was lost among the needles. Fear filled his gut like a meal he could not digest. He whispered a prayer to the nameless gods of the wood, and slipped his dirk free of its sheath. He put it between his teeth to keep both hands free for climbing. The taste of cold iron in his mouth gave him comfort.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will停止争辩，攀树前拔出短刀；寒铁的味道给他一点心理安慰。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 短刀的味觉细节把读者拉回Will的身体，也显示他只能依赖微小、熟悉的实物获得安全感。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Down below, the lordling called out suddenly, “Who goes there?” Will heard uncertainty in the challenge. He stopped climbing; he listened; he watched.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 下方的Waymar突然挑战未知者；Will从他的声音里听出了不确定。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段利用空营地、消失的尸体和人物反应，把预感转化为可观察的异常。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: The woods gave answer: the rustle of leaves, the icy rush of the stream, a distant hoot of a snow owl.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 树林只传来树叶、溪水和雪鸮等自然声音。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 自然界有声，异鬼无声；沉默因此不再是空白，而成为它们的标志。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** 本段第一次把这些存在称为 `the Others`。中文常译“异鬼”；在当前进度只应把它视为未知、非人的北方威胁。

blocks.original_text: The Others made no sound.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 真正的“异鬼”却完全无声。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段主要依靠声音、温度、颜色和运动方式描写未知生物，保留解释空白。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Will saw movement from the corner of his eye. Pale shapes gliding through the wood. He turned his head, glimpsed a white shadow in the darkness. Then it was gone. Branches stirred gently in the wind, scratching at one another with wooden fingers. Will opened his mouth to call down a warning, and the words seemed to freeze in his throat. Perhaps he was wrong. Perhaps it had only been a bird, a reflection on the snow, some trick of the moonlight. What had he seen, after all?
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will瞥见苍白身影在林间滑行，甲胄颜色随环境变化，使它几乎无法被锁定。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `gliding` 和会变色的甲胄让它们不像普通步行的人，也解释了Will为何难以持续看清。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: “Will, where are you?” Ser Waymar called up. “Can you see anything?” He was turning in a slow circle, suddenly wary, his sword in hand. He must have felt them, as Will felt them. There was nothing to see. “Answer me! Why is it so cold?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar在空地里转身搜寻，终于放弃从容姿态，公开呼唤Will。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段主要依靠声音、温度、颜色和运动方式描写未知生物，保留解释空白。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: It was cold. Shivering, Will clung more tightly to his perch. His face pressed hard against the trunk of the sentinel. He could feel the sweet, sticky sap on his cheek.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 严寒骤然加强；树液似乎冻结，Will嘴边的呼吸结霜，说明这不是普通夜寒。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 骤降的温度与异鬼出现同步，把Gared早先关于“真正敌人是寒冷”的话转化为现实。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: A shadow emerged from the dark of the wood. It stood in front of Royce. Tall, it was, and gaunt and hard as old bones, with flesh pale as milk. Its armor seemed to change color as it moved; here it was white as new-fallen snow, there black as shadow, everywhere dappled with the deep grey-green of the trees. The patterns ran like moonlight on water with every step it took.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 一个高瘦、苍白、仿佛由旧骨构成的身影走到Waymar面前。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 描写避免直接给出完整答案，只以骨、乳白与肉的比喻拼出非人的身体感。
blocks.background_note_zh: **当前可知：** 异鬼具有实体、装备和主动行动能力；关于来源、种族和目的，序章尚无答案。

blocks.original_text: Will heard the breath go out of Ser Waymar Royce in a long hiss. “Come no farther,” the lordling warned. His voice cracked like a boy’s. He threw the long sable cloak back over his shoulders, to free his arms for battle, and took his sword in both hands. The wind had stopped. It was very cold.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar虽然声音发颤，仍拔剑摆出战斗姿态；他的斗篷暴露在月光下。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段主要依靠声音、温度、颜色和运动方式描写未知生物，保留解释空白。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: The Other slid forward on silent feet. In its hand was a longsword like none that Will had ever seen. No human metal had gone into the forging of that blade. It was alive with moonlight, translucent, a shard of crystal so thin that it seemed almost to vanish when seen edge-on. There was a faint blue shimmer to the thing, a ghost-light that played around its edges, and somehow Will knew it was sharper than any razor.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 异鬼无声逼近，手中的剑呈半透明晶体状，极薄且泛着幽蓝光。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 异鬼之剑同时像晶体、冰和光；作者用互相矛盾的材质感强调它不属于普通锻造体系。
blocks.background_note_zh: **当前可知：** 异鬼武器并非Will熟悉的人类金属剑；其材料与制造方式仍未知。

blocks.original_text: Ser Waymar met him bravely. “Dance with me then.” He lifted his sword high over his head, defiant. His hands trembled from the weight of it, or perhaps from the cold. Yet in that moment, Will thought, he was a boy no longer, but a man of the Night’s Watch.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar勇敢应战，用“与我共舞”发出挑战；这一刻他真正成为守夜人战士。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Waymar此前的傲慢没有被洗白，但在真正危险面前，他确实选择迎战，人物获得悲剧性的尊严。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: The Other halted. Will saw its eyes; blue, deeper and bluer than any human eyes, a blue that burned like ice. They fixed on the longsword trembling on high, watched the moonlight running cold along the metal. For a heartbeat he dared to hope.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 异鬼观察Waymar的剑，眼睛像燃烧的冰；Will短暂希望它也会畏惧人类武器。
blocks.reading_note_zh: “冰在燃烧”是矛盾修辞，把极寒写成具有主动能量的存在。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: They emerged silently from the shadows, twins to the first. Three of them . . . four . . . five . . . Ser Waymar may have felt the cold that came with them, but he never saw them, never heard them. Will had to call out. It was his duty. And his death, if he did. He shivered, and hugged the tree, and kept the silence.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 更多异鬼无声包围Waymar；Will明白出声既是职责，也几乎等于送死，最终选择沉默。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Will在职责与求生之间选择沉默；文本不简单裁决他，而让读者感受这个选择的代价。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: The pale sword came shivering through the air.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 异鬼率先挥出苍白长剑。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段用短句和感官比喻加快战斗节奏，并持续强调人类武器的劣势。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Ser Waymar met it with steel. When the blades met, there was no ring of metal on metal; only a high, thin sound at the edge of hearing, like an animal screaming in pain. Royce checked a second blow, and a third, then fell back a step. Another flurry of blows, and he fell back again.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 两剑相击没有金属声，而发出近似动物痛苦的尖鸣；Waymar因此感到恐惧。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 声音先于伤口暴露力量差异：人类钢铁面对异鬼武器时，连熟悉的金属规则都失效。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Behind him, to right, to left, all around him, the watchers stood patient, faceless, silent, the shifting patterns of their delicate armor making them all but invisible in the wood. Yet they made no move to interfere.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 其他异鬼像观众一样耐心围观，甲胄继续随林影变化。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 围观姿态暗示这不是势均力敌的战斗，更像观察、试探或仪式性的猎杀。后两种属于阅读推测。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Again and again the swords met, until Will wanted to cover his ears against the strange anguished keening of their clash. Ser Waymar was panting from the effort now, his breath steaming in the moonlight. His blade was white with frost; the Other’s danced with pale blue light.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 连续交锋使Waymar精疲力竭、流血，铠甲结霜，战斗声也越来越尖厉。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段用短句和感官比喻加快战斗节奏，并持续强调人类武器的劣势。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Then Royce’s parry came a beat too late. The pale sword bit through the ringmail beneath his arm. The young lord cried out in pain. Blood welled between the rings. It steamed in the cold, and the droplets seemed red as fire where they touched the snow. Ser Waymar’s fingers brushed his side. His moleskin glove came away soaked with red.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar一次格挡稍迟，异鬼的剑穿过环甲，在腋下割伤他。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段用短句和感官比喻加快战斗节奏，并持续强调人类武器的劣势。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: The Other said something in a language that Will did not know; his voice was like the cracking of ice on a winter lake, and the words were mocking.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 异鬼用未知语言说话并似乎嘲弄；它的声音被比作冬湖冰裂。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 异鬼不仅能行动，也有语言和类似笑声的交流，这使其比无意识怪物更令人不安。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Ser Waymar Royce found his fury. “For Robert!” he shouted, and he came up snarling, lifting the frost-covered longsword with both hands and swinging it around in a flat sidearm slash with all his weight behind it. The Other’s parry was almost lazy.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar因愤怒重新振作，高喊效忠国王Robert并发动最后攻击。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `For Robert` 表明Waymar在绝境中仍以王国与誓约定义自己。此处不展开Robert的后续背景。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Robert 是Waymar效忠的国王。为保持无剧透，本篇不补充其政治史。

blocks.original_text: When the blades touched, the steel shattered.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 两剑再次相撞，人类钢剑瞬间碎裂。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 单句成段让“钢剑碎裂”成为全场最干脆的力量判决。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: A scream echoed through the forest night, and the longsword shivered into a hundred brittle pieces, the shards scattering like a rain of needles. Royce went to his knees, shrieking, and covered his eyes. Blood welled between his fingers.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 碎片像针雨飞散，一片刺入Waymar左眼；他跪地惨叫。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段用短句和感官比喻加快战斗节奏，并持续强调人类武器的劣势。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: The watchers moved forward together, as if some signal had been given. Swords rose and fell, all in a deathly silence. It was cold butchery. The pale blades sliced through ringmail as if it were silk. Will closed his eyes. Far beneath him, he heard their voices and laughter sharp as icicles.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 围观者一同上前，以绝对安静的方式反复砍杀Waymar，随后发出冰冷笑声。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `cold butchery` 既指残酷屠杀，也把“冷”从温度扩展为施暴者的无情。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: When he found the courage to look again, a long time had passed, and the ridge below was empty.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 许久之后，Will才敢重新睁眼；山脊下已经空无一物。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在战斗后的寂静中制造第二次反转，使开篇关于死亡的争论获得残酷回应。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: He stayed in the tree, scarce daring to breathe, while the moon crept slowly across the black sky. Finally, his muscles cramping and his fingers numb with cold, he climbed down.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will在树上等到月亮移动很远，终于因肌肉僵硬和Gared的缺席而决定下去。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在战斗后的寂静中制造第二次反转，使开篇关于死亡的争论获得残酷回应。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Royce’s body lay facedown in the snow, one arm outflung. The thick sable cloak had been slashed in a dozen places. Lying dead like that, you saw how young he was. A boy.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Waymar的尸体俯卧雪中，华丽斗篷和精良环甲都被轻易破坏。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在战斗后的寂静中制造第二次反转，使开篇关于死亡的争论获得残酷回应。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: He found what was left of the sword a few feet away, the end splintered and twisted like a tree struck by lightning. Will knelt, looked around warily, and snatched it up. The broken sword would be his proof. Gared would know what to make of it, and if not him, then surely that old bear Mormont or Maester Aemon. Would Gared still be waiting with the horses? He had to hurry.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will捡起断剑残片，发现其边缘薄得像削刃；这可作为异常遭遇的物证。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Will想带走证物，说明他仍希望让不可置信的经历进入可验证的现实。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: Will rose. Ser Waymar Royce stood over him.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Will起身时，本应死亡的Waymar已经站在他身后。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 生与死的界线在一个五词短句中被推翻，呼应开头Gared的 `Dead is dead`。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: His fine clothes were a tatter, his face a ruin. A shard from his sword transfixed the blind white pupil of his left eye.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 复起的Waymar衣脸尽毁、左眼被碎片贯穿，右手仍握着断剑。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段在战斗后的寂静中制造第二次反转，使开篇关于死亡的争论获得残酷回应。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

blocks.original_text: The right eye was open. The pupil burned blue. It saw.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 他睁开的右眼已经燃烧着与异鬼相同的蓝光，并且正在注视Will。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 眼睛颜色成为状态改变的明确视觉证据；文本只展示变化，不在序章内解释机制。
blocks.background_note_zh: **当前可知：** 蓝眼与复起同时出现，表明Waymar已发生超自然改变；本篇不提前命名或解释该状态。

blocks.original_text: The broken sword fell from nerveless fingers. Will closed his eyes to pray. Long, elegant hands brushed his cheek, then tightened around his throat. They were gloved in the finest moleskin and sticky with blood, yet the touch was icy cold.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 断剑落下，Will闭眼祈祷；Waymar冰冷的双手随即扼住他的喉咙。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 结尾不直接写死亡结果，而停在触觉与窒息动作上，使Will的命运在感官层面封闭。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；理解重点在人物反应与现场信息。

glossary.note: 奇幻专名，在人物话语中带有边境内部人的称呼色彩
比普通 disagreement 更带情绪冲突
替别人的婴儿哺乳并照料孩子的女性
古风且带性别羞辱意味
`barely suppressed anger` 指几乎压不住的愤怒

glossary.translation: 危险的；接近危险程度的
glossary.note: `come perilous close to` 表示危险地接近
比 fear 更含模糊、未明的担忧
生动的身体化恐惧表达
本书中守夜人的任务用语
`too many heirs` 暗示幼子继承机会有限
比普通乘用马高大昂贵
适合崎岖地形的结实小马
经处理后变硬，可作护甲
贵重毛皮，也强化全黑形象
未经许可猎取他人土地上的猎物
通常倚靠岩石、墙或树搭建
从语境可知是负责观察远处的人

glossary.translation: 头发灰白的；饱经风霜的
职业武装侍从；披甲战士
glossary.note: 并不必然拥有骑士头衔
此处指冻伤后缺失耳朵留下的部位
文学化用法，`clad in fur and leather`
常用于 `deign to do`，带讽刺
常形容古树或老人的手
比冷淡更明确地表现优越感
没有真正赞同，只是不再反抗
此处把树拟人化为守卫
常带不健康、骷髅般的感觉
比 killing 更强调冷酷和血腥
常指玻璃、金属或陶器碎片
